text.title: Lightweight Single-Pass Persona Building
text.summary: Single-pass persona generation pipeline for early exploration: Sonnet builds the card from structured input, Haiku formats it; human review is the only validation gate.

list.item: 1. Supply structured input: user type, at least 3 concrete data points, JTBD statement
2. Sonnet subagent generates persona card; marks all inferred fields as [INFERRED]
3. Haiku subagent formats card into team documentation template (no content changes)
4. Human review: smell-test against real users known to the team; approve before use

rule.statement: Supply at least 3 concrete data points per persona — zero-data prompts produce archetype stereotypes.
rule.rationale: Without grounding data, LLMs default to demographic stereotypes rather than behavioral segments. Even thin evidence (3 facts) significantly improves output quality.

rule.statement: Require the LLM to tag all inferred fields explicitly; teams often treat untagged output as validated fact.
rule.rationale: AI-generated fields that look like facts get used as facts. The [INFERRED] tag creates a visible validation queue.

rule.statement: Limit each persona to one primary goal; multi-goal personas produce unfocused design decisions.
rule.rationale: When a persona has multiple primary goals, designers optimize for the wrong one or try to satisfy all of them, producing diluted solutions.

rule.statement: Store the prompt and input alongside the output so personas can be regenerated as data improves.
rule.rationale: Persona generation is not a one-time artifact. Storing the full input enables reproducible updates when new research data arrives.

rule.statement: Use this lightweight method only for early exploration; graduate to ai-assisted-persona-building once real cluster data exists.
rule.rationale: This pipeline has no clustering step. Once enough behavioral data exists (30+ records per segment), the full clustering pipeline produces more reliable, segmented personas.

text.reference: Claude API call: user_type + data_points + jtbd → persona card
Sonnet prompt enforcing data-only output with [INFERRED] tagging
Haiku formatting prompt for team template population
